memoryArtifacts.description:
BIO:  from Eyton's Antiquities of Shropshire, Vol. X, p. 82 (Corbet of Wattlesborough and Moreton-Corbet pedigree chart), 

shows Peter Fitz Torret married to Lucia Haget, the daughter of Betram Haget …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Joanna de Toret於1195年出生在英國英格蘭史洛普爾莫雷頓 可特時，她的父親Bartholomew de Toret26歲，母親Agnes31歲。她於大約1201年與Richard Corbet在英國英格蘭史洛普爾華特萊斯波洛結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1239年之前在她的家鄉。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Toret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alain, Alcide, Antoine, Camille, Emile, Anatole, Armand, Chantal, Clemile, Clovis, Gillis.
French (Forêt): variant of Forest ‘forest’ and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Forest
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Pascal, Amie, Armand, Bertice, Etienne, Fernand, Germaine, Jacques, Normand, Sylvain.

French: topographic name for someone who lived in or near a royal forest, or a metonymic occupational name for a keeper or worker in one, from Old French forest ‘forest’; or a habitational name from (La) Forest, the name of several places in various parts of France. Unlike modern French forêt, forest was not simply a word for extensive woodland, but referred specifically to large wooded areas reserved by law for the king and his nobles to hunt in (see also English Forrest ). Compare Deforest and Laforest .
English and Scottish: variant of   Forrest  , a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Joanna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form, Io(h)anna, of Greek Iōanna, the feminine equivalent of Iōannēs ( see John ). In the New Testament, this name is borne by a woman who was one of Jesus's followers (Luke 8:3; 24:10). She was the wife of the steward of the household of King Herod Antipas. The name was regularly used throughout the Middle Ages in most parts of Europe as a feminine equivalent of John , but in England it has only been in common use as a vernacular given name since the 19th century. Celebrated British bearers of the name include the novelist Joanna Trollope ( b. 1943 ), the actress Joanna Lumley ( b. 1946 ), and the concert pianist Joanna McGregor ( b. 1959 ).

variants.name: Joannah
meanings: From the Latin form, Io(h)anna, of Greek Iōanna, the feminine equivalent of Iōannēs ( see John ). In the New Testament, this name is borne by a woman who was one of Jesus's followers (Luke 8:3; 24:10). She was the wife of the steward of the household of King Herod Antipas. The name was regularly used throughout the Middle Ages in most parts of Europe as a feminine equivalent of John , but in England it has only been in common use as a vernacular given name since the 19th century. Celebrated British bearers of the name include the novelist Joanna Trollope ( b. 1943 ), the actress Joanna Lumley ( b. 1946 ), and the concert pianist Joanna McGregor ( b. 1959 ).

meanings: From the Latin form, Io(h)anna, of Greek Iōanna, the feminine equivalent of Iōannēs ( see John ). In the New Testament, this name is borne by a woman who was one of Jesus's followers (Luke 8:3; 24:10). She was the wife of the steward of the household of King Herod Antipas. The name was regularly used throughout the Middle Ages in most parts of Europe as a feminine equivalent of John , but in England it has only been in common use as a vernacular given name since the 19th century. Celebrated British bearers of the name include the novelist Joanna Trollope ( b. 1943 ), the actress Joanna Lumley ( b. 1946 ), and the concert pianist Joanna McGregor ( b. 1959 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Joanna de Toret was born in 1195, in Moreton Corbet, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Bartholomew de Toret, was 26 and her mother, Agnes, was 31. She married Richard Corbet about 1201, in Wattlesborough, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died before 1239, in her hometown.

Elle a épousé Richard Corbet environ 1201, à Wattlesborough, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée avant 1239, dans sa ville natale.

Lei sposato Richard Corbet circa 1201, nel Wattlesborough, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì prima del 1239, nel sua cittadina.

ko.Moreton Toret,,Shropshire,England: 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 모레턴 비 오스베스트리
ko.lifeSummary: Joanna de Toret(이)가 1195년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 모레턴 코벳에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Bartholomew de Toret(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 대략 1201년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 왓틀레스버루에서 Richard Corbet(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1239년 이전 그녀의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joanna de Toret nasceu em 1195, em Moreton Corbet, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Bartholomew de Toret, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Agnes, tinha 31 anos. Ela casou-se com Richard Corbet aproximadamente 1201, em Wattlesborough, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu antes 1239, em sua cidade natal.

ru.Moreton Toret,,Shropshire,England: моретон би освестри, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joanna de Toret родилась в 1195 г. в местности моретон корбет, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Bartholomew de Toret было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Corbet около 1201 г. в местности ваттлесбороую, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла перед 1239 г. в её родном городе.
